title: BitSight Finding Risk Vectors - Exploited, Malware, SSL, Open Ports, And Patching
applies_to: Extracting granular BitSight findings per risk vector for detailed vendor assessments
Building per-category risk breakdowns in Power BI (Compromised Systems, Diligence, User Behavior)
Prioritising vendor remediation requests based on specific finding types

confirmed_facts.fact: Findings endpoint: `GET https://api.bitsighttech.com/ratings/v1/companies/{guid}/findings`. Returns paginated findings for a company. Each finding includes `risk_vector` (category slug), `risk_vector_label` (display name), `severity` (minor/moderate/material/severe), `details`, `assets` (affected IPs/domains), `first_seen`, `last_seen`, and `remaining_count`.

Risk vector category groups: Compromised Systems (botnet_infections, spam_propagation, malware_servers, potentially_exploited), Diligence (ssl_certificates, ssl_configurations, open_ports, web_application_headers, patching_cadence, insecure_systems, application_security, server_software, dkim, spf), User Behavior (file_sharing), Public Disclosures (data_breaches). Each category contributes a sub-score to the overall rating.

Filter findings by risk vector: `GET .../findings?risk_vector=potentially_exploited`. Returns only findings in that risk vector. High-value risk vectors for security reports: `potentially_exploited` (active compromise indicators), `ssl_configurations` (weak ciphers/protocols), `open_ports` (exposed attack surface), `patching_cadence` (unpatched software).

Filter by severity: `GET .../findings?severity=severe,material`. Severity levels from highest to lowest: severe, material, moderate, minor. For vendor risk assessments, focus on severe and material findings. Add `&risk_vector=potentially_exploited` to further narrow to the most critical active compromise signals.

Finding `assets` field: an array of affected asset objects with `asset` (IP or domain string), `asset_type` ('ip' or 'domain'), `importance` ('high'/'medium'/'low'), and in some risk vectors, `port`, `protocol`, and `evidence` fields containing technical details (e.g., SSL cipher name, open port number, malware family name).

Aggregate findings count per risk vector for a company: the `factors` array in the company detail response (`GET /ratings/v1/companies/{guid}`) already contains the per-risk-vector score and finding count without needing to call the findings endpoint. Use factors for summary metrics; use findings for detailed evidence.

implementation_notes: For Power BI dashboards: store finding counts per risk vector per company from the `factors` array (available in every company detail call). Only call the findings endpoint when a user drills through to see individual finding evidence — this avoids making hundreds of per-company finding API calls in the daily pipeline.

For vendor remediation letters: extract severe/material findings per risk vector for the target company, format as a structured table (Risk Vector, Severity, Asset, First Seen, Evidence), and include in the report email or SharePoint document.

Map BitSight risk vector slugs to display-friendly names for reports: `$rvLabels = @{ 'potentially_exploited'='Potentially Exploited'; 'ssl_configurations'='SSL/TLS Configuration'; 'open_ports'='Open Ports'; 'patching_cadence'='Patching Cadence' }`. Use this hashtable for label substitution when building SharePoint list rows or Power BI dimension tables.
caveats: Finding data available via the API depends on the BitSight subscription tier. Some risk vectors (e.g., potentially_exploited) require higher-tier subscriptions. Verify available risk vectors by calling the findings endpoint without filters and inspecting the returned risk_vector values.
The findings API can return large result sets for companies with many findings. Always paginate via links.next and set a practical per_page limit (e.g., 100) to manage response size.

Finding `last_seen` dates indicate when BitSight last observed the finding. Findings not observed in recent scans may still appear in the API with an older `last_seen`. Consider findings with `last_seen` older than 30 days as potentially resolved but not yet confirmed.
